instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertions on How to Decide Coverage Under the Group Life Insurance Policy- Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: group insurance, group insurance policy, group life insurance policy
Body Content:
Employers offer a number of benefits to employees of any given organisation. This is done with a view to maintain a talent pool of staff and to display the company’s appreciation for certain employees. One benefit that any employer can offer to an employee is a group insurance policy. This is a form of life insurance in which a single contract is made available by an employer, to cover many employees (hence, the word “group”). The contract is given to the employer who then offers it to employees as a benefit.
As you well know, the world we live in today is rife with unpredictable circumstances, and financial security is of the utmost importance. It has become even more vital to secure the future of your family members and near and dear ones in the event that you may face an unfortunate circumstance and you are no longer around. With costs of every product and service, including healthcare, rising exponentially, having a financial safeguard is necessary. A group insurance policy is taken be the employer on behalf of the employee, and this ensures that a nominee/beneficiary listed in the policy receives a lump sum as a death benefit This is done should any unfortunate event, i.e. the demise of the employee, takes place during the tenure of the policy However, you may be wondering how an employer decides on coverage when there is a group insurance policy in question.
How to Decide Coverage under the Group Life Insurance Policy
The amount of coverage for employees in a group is largely determined by classes/benefit schedules that employees are categorised by. This makes it easy for an employer to decide on the coverage value. An employer may use any of the different benefit schedules below:
A Schedule of Earnings - While considering the coverage of a group life insurance policy, employers classify employees into different categories based on the salaries they earn (excluding bonuses). This is a method that is frequently used by employers to decide the coverage. The amount is determined by a percentage of the salary earned. A multiple of the annual salary of any employee may be used. To the executive class of employees, higher multiples are considered, based on salaries.
A Flat Benefit Schedule - Here, no consideration is given to the designation or the salary of an employee. Instead, the organisation’s management views all the employees as equal, grouping them in a single class. Consequently, all employees receive identical benefits under such a group insurance plan.
A Combination Schedule - Companies are entitled to use a blend of two schedules to decide coverage. For instance, employers can make use of the earnings schedule for deciding on benefits to the most valued company staff. However, if the same company has certain staff working on an hourly basis, the flat benefit schedule may be employed for that portion of the staff.
A Schedule Based on Length of Service - This method of coverage determination is used by few companies today. Here, coverage decisions are based on the tenure of service of employees. For instance, employees who have given long-term services to the company may get more coverage through a group insurance policy.
A Case on Group Life Cover Decision
MANUCO is an organisation in the manufacturing industry. This company has two kinds of employees; those who are employed on an hourly basis in factories, and those who are salaried employees working in the administrative, accounting, sales, research, etc, divisions of the same organisation. This second category of staff are full-time employees. They have salaries that are fixed, plus get incentives. MANUCO wishes to purchase group insurance for all its employees. To decide how much coverage the organisation must purchase, employees are categorised in the following way:
Permanent employees responsible for company operations
Temporary employees who work in the company’s factories
Furthermore, the organisation divided permanent employees into entry-level employees, mid-level employees, and senior employees to decide coverage based on a percentage of each class’ annual salary (an average was used here). For temporary employees, the same benefit of cover was given across all staff, irrespective of the number of hours of employment.
Conclusion
If you are a salaried employee, a group life insurance policy can benefit you. As an earning member of your family, you can ensure you get peace of mind as you know that your loved ones will receive a death benefit should you succumb to any unforeseen circumstance. Although you may think you can financially safeguard your family’s future with pay-outs from gratuity, provident fund, etc, these amounts are negligible in case of a premature death. Hence, group term life insurance is the need of the hour.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
Sources:
https://securenow.in/insuropedia/how-to-decide-coverage-under-the-group-life-insurance-policy/
Group Insurance Policy With Employee Benefits | Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance
